skill.name: amplitude-automation
skill.description: Automate Amplitude product analytics workflows through Rube MCP, including event ingestion, user lookup, user properties, and cohort operations.
skill.summary: Use Rube MCP to run common Amplitude analytics workflows from an AI coding assistant.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static external command, weak crypto, role token, and reconnaissance alerts are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, Amplitude field names, and workflow text in SKILL.md. The confirmed risk is legitimate network use through Rube MCP and Amplitude actions that can send events, update user properties, and change cohort membership.

medium_findings.title: External MCP Connection Controls Amplitude Data
medium_findings.description: The skill directs users to connect Rube MCP at https://rube.app/mcp and use Amplitude tools. This is expected behavior, but it sends analytics data to external services and can modify user properties and cohort membership, so users must authorize the connection carefully.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly configure an external MCP endpoint and list Amplitude operations that write events, identify users, and update cohorts. The behavior appears legitimate for the skill purpose, but it affects external analytics data.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The repeated Ruby or shell backtick findings refer to inline Markdown code spans and fenced examples for tool names, parameters, and Amplitude field names. No executable script, shell invocation, or command interpolation was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these locations are documentation examples and inline code formatting. There is no runnable Ruby, shell command, or script file in the skill.

low_findings.title: Role Token, Weak Crypto, and Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The role token alert occurs in parameter documentation for FIND_USER, and the reconnaissance alerts occur in Amplitude ID and status workflow text. The weak crypto alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code or hashing instructions in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text is ordinary skill documentation about Amplitude users, identifiers, API access, and workflow status. No prompt override text, cryptographic operation, or host/network probing instruction was found.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast access to Amplitude actions without switching tools or guessing API schemas. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Rube MCP workflows for events, users, cohorts, and activity lookup.
actual_capabilities: Searches current Rube MCP tool schemas before calling Amplitude tools.
Sends Amplitude events with user, device, timestamp, event, and user property fields.
Finds users and retrieves user activity with the correct Amplitude internal user ID.
Updates Amplitude user properties through IDENTIFY operations.
Lists cohorts, retrieves cohort details, updates memberships, and checks async status.
Lists event categories to help validate event names before ingestion.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP availability and an active Amplitude connection.
Does not include its own Amplitude API client or offline analytics engine.
Successful event ingestion may not make data immediately queryable in Amplitude.
Large cohort changes may require batching and status polling.

use_cases.title: Instrument product events
use_cases.description: Send validated events with the right user identifiers, timestamps, and properties through Amplitude.

use_cases.title: Inspect customer activity
use_cases.description: Resolve an application user ID to an Amplitude user and review recent activity.

use_cases.title: Maintain analytics cohorts
use_cases.description: List cohorts, update cohort membership, and poll status for async membership changes.

prompt_templates.title: Check the connection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Amplitude automation skill to confirm Rube MCP is connected, search for current Amplitude tools, and verify that my Amplitude connection is active.

prompt_templates.title: Send a product event
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the current Amplitude tool schema, then send a signup_completed event for user ID USER_ID with plan and source properties. Confirm the timestamp format before sending.

prompt_templates.title: Review user activity
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the Amplitude user for application user ID USER_ID, retrieve recent activity, and summarize the latest important events with pagination if needed.

prompt_templates.title: Update a cohort safely
prompt_templates.prompt: List cohorts, find the cohort named COHORT_NAME, add these user IDs to it, then poll the async status until the update completes or reports an error.

output_examples.input: Send a purchase event for one user.
output_examples.output: The assistant confirms the current schema, checks that a user ID or device ID is present, uses a millisecond timestamp, sends the event, and reports the Amplitude response status.

output_examples.input: Show recent activity for a customer.
output_examples.output: The assistant finds the matching Amplitude user, retrieves recent events, and summarizes activity in reverse chronological order with any pagination notes.

output_examples.input: Add users to a saved cohort.
output_examples.output: The assistant finds the cohort ID, submits membership additions, captures the request ID, and checks completion status before reporting the result.

best_practices: Always search Rube MCP tools first because schemas and limits can change.
Confirm user identifiers and timestamps before sending events or property updates.
Poll async cohort operations and report incomplete or error states clearly.
anti_patterns: Do not send events without a user ID or device ID.
Do not use application user IDs where Amplitude internal user IDs are required.
Do not assume a successful ingestion response means data is immediately queryable.

faq.question: Does this skill require Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Yes. It depends on Rube MCP and an active Amplitude connection before any workflow can run.

faq.question: Can it send new Amplitude events?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide event ingestion through AMPLITUDE_SEND_EVENTS after checking the current schema.

faq.question: Can it look up user activity?
faq.answer: Yes. It first resolves the user with AMPLITUDE_FIND_USER, then calls AMPLITUDE_GET_USER_ACTIVITY.

faq.question: Can it update user properties?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports IDENTIFY workflows with Amplitude property operations such as set, add, append, and unset.

faq.question: Can it manage cohorts?
faq.answer: Yes. It can list cohorts, get cohort details, update membership, and check async update status.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it connects to external services and can modify analytics data.
